page: Microsoft Azure FX16-8mds_v2 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the FXmdsv2 family, designed for memory-intensive workloads. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz, configured with 4 physical cores and 8 virtual CPUs (vCPUs) via Microsoft Hyper-V virtualization. The system is equipped with 336.0 GB of system memory, yielding an allocation of 42.0 GB per physical core. Local storage is provided via a bundled 945 GB local disk, and the instance is Premium Storage capable.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2165.0 and a single-threaded gzip compression speed of 34.31 GB/sec. Text processing performance is highly competitive, achieving a top-tier Geekbench text processing score of 2931.0. Multi-core performance is moderate, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 8882.55 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 7546.0. In database operations, the server handles 12.07 million Redis SET operations per second.

Despite the massive memory capacity, memory performance presents some tradeoffs. The Passmark memory score is relatively weak at 1324.99, and large-block uncached read memory bandwidth is measured at 44.97 GB/sec. Additionally, while the CPU supports Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), LLM inference text generation for a medium 7B model is limited to a weak 8.54 tokens/sec. From a cost-efficiency perspective, this server offers a highly specialized resource profile, providing a massive memory-to-vCPU ratio and high clock-speed processing without the overhead of a high vCPU count, making it a qualitatively efficient option for memory-bound but core-limited software licensing models.

The Microsoft Azure FX16-8mds_v2 is suited for memory-intensive applications, large relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, text processing pipelines, and workloads requiring high single-core CPU performance coupled with a substantial memory footprint.
description: The Microsoft Azure FX16-8mds_v2 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server from the FXmdsv2 family, powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 4 physical cores and 8 vCPUs. It features an exceptionally high memory capacity of 336.0 GB (42.0 GB per core) and includes a 945 GB local disk with Premium Storage capabilities. Benchmark results show strong single-core performance, highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 2165.0 and top-tier text processing capabilities. However, memory-specific benchmarks reveal some tradeoffs, such as a weak Passmark memory score of 1324.99. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring massive memory density relative to CPU cores, making it highly suitable for in-memory databases, caching layers, and memory-bound enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX16-8mds_v2 is a memory-optimized server featuring an 8-vCPU Intel Xeon processor, 336 GB of RAM, and a 945 GB local disk, delivering strong single-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure FX16-8mds_v2 is an 8-vCPU memory-optimized server with 336 GB RAM and 945 GB local storage, built for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A high-frequency, memory-dense server featuring a massive memory-to-core ratio and local storage for demanding database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 8 vCPUs running at 4.2 GHz
Massive 336.0 GB memory capacity providing 42.0 GB per core
Bundled 945 GB local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong single-core performance and top-tier text processing benchmarks
Optimized for in-memory databases, caching, and memory-bound workloads
